name: Claude 3 Sonnet
tagline: The original balanced Sonnet that launched the Claude 3 Opus/Sonnet/Haiku family.
metaDescription: Claude 3 Sonnet (Mar 2024) launched Anthropic's Opus/Sonnet/Haiku tiers: 79.0% MMLU, 73.0% HumanEval, 200K context, vision support and $3/$15 per MTok pricing.
architecture: Proprietary transformer (non-reasoning); first model in the Claude 3 family's middle tier.
note: Launched the Sonnet tier (claude-3-sonnet-20240229) — the original balanced model in the Claude 3 Opus/Sonnet/Haiku family.
overview: Claude 3 Sonnet is the original Sonnet model, announced on March 4, 2024 as the balanced middle tier of the Claude 3 family, between Claude 3 Opus and Claude 3 Haiku. It introduced the Opus/Sonnet/Haiku naming that every later Claude generation has used.

It was the first Claude model with vision, able to process photos, charts, graphs and technical diagrams alongside text. On standard benchmarks it scored 79.0% on MMLU, 40.4% on GPQA, 73.0% on HumanEval, and 92.3% on GSM8K, positioning it as a strong, affordable model for scaled enterprise deployments.

Claude 3 Sonnet has a 200K token context window, up to 4,096 output tokens, an August 2023 knowledge cutoff, and supports text and image input, at $3 / $15 per million tokens. Its model ID is claude-3-sonnet-20240229; the snapshot was retired on July 21, 2025.
strengths: Introduced the Opus/Sonnet/Haiku tier naming for the Claude family
First Claude model with vision (charts, diagrams, photos)
200K token context window at an affordable mid-tier price
Balanced intelligence and speed for enterprise scale
Solid math and coding scores (92.3% GSM8K, 73.0% HumanEval)
useCases: Vision tasks over charts, graphs and technical diagrams
Knowledge-base Q&A and enterprise content workflows
Cost-sensitive deployments needing a balanced model
Math and coding assistance at moderate difficulty

faq.a: It was announced on March 4, 2024 as the balanced middle tier of the Claude 3 family (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku).
79.0% on MMLU, 40.4% on GPQA, 73.0% on HumanEval, and 92.3% on GSM8K.
200K token context window, up to 4,096 output tokens, an August 2023 knowledge cutoff, text and vision input, at $3/$15 per million tokens.
No. The claude-3-sonnet-20240229 snapshot was retired on July 21, 2025. Anthropic recommends Claude Sonnet 4.6 as the replacement.
